root_text.an4.166:2.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yāyaṁ paṭipadā dukkhā dandhābhiññā, ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭipadā ubhayeneva hīnā akkhāyati. Yampāyaṁ paṭipadā dukkhā, imināpāyaṁ hīnā akkhāyati; yampāyaṁ paṭipadā dandhā, imināpāyaṁ hīnā akkhāyati.
root_text.an4.166:2.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭipadā ubhayeneva hīnā akkhāyati.
root_text.an4.166:3.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yāyaṁ paṭipadā dukkhā khippābhiññā, ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭipadā dukkhattā hīnā akkhāyati.
root_text.an4.166:4.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yāyaṁ paṭipadā sukhā dandhābhiññā, ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭipadā dandhattā hīnā akkhāyati.
root_text.an4.166:5.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yāyaṁ paṭipadā sukhā khippābhiññā, ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭipadā ubhayeneva paṇītā akkhāyati. Yampāyaṁ paṭipadā sukhā, imināpāyaṁ paṇītā akkhāyati; yampāyaṁ paṭipadā khippā, imināpāyaṁ paṇītā akkhāyati.
root_text.an4.166:5.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭipadā ubhayeneva paṇītā akkhāyati.

translation_text.an4.166:1.1: “Mendicants, there are four ways of practice.
translation_text.an4.166:1.5: pleasant practice with slow insight, and
translation_text.an4.166:2.1: Of these, the painful practice with slow insight is said to be inferior in both ways: because it’s painful and because it’s slow.
translation_text.an4.166:2.2: This practice is said to be inferior in both ways.
translation_text.an4.166:3.1: The painful practice with swift insight is said to be inferior because it’s painful.
translation_text.an4.166:4.1: The pleasant practice with slow insight is said to be inferior because it’s slow.
translation_text.an4.166:5.1: The pleasant practice with swift insight is said to be superior in both ways: because it’s pleasant, and because it’s swift.
translation_text.an4.166:5.2: This practice is said to be superior in both ways.
translation_text.an4.166:5.3: These are the four ways of practice.”
